title: Analog Devices appoints tech innovator Yoky Matsuoka to board
summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADI) has appointed Dr. Yoky Matsuoka, a technology innovator and executive officer at Panasonic Holdings, to its Board of Directors as an independent director, effective January 20, 2026. Matsuoka, recognized for her expertise in integrating hardware, software, and AI, previously held leadership roles at Google, Apple, and Quanttus. Her addition expands ADI's board to 11 members as the semiconductor company continues to strengthen its leadership and financial position, with recent positive analyst upgrades and a 23-year streak of dividend increases.

title: Analog Devices Appoints Yoky Matsuoka to its Board of Directors
summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (Nasdaq: ADI) has announced the appointment of Dr. Yoky Matsuoka to its Board of Directors as an independent director and a member of the Corporate Development Committee, effective January 20, 2026. Dr. Matsuoka, an executive officer at Panasonic Holdings with extensive experience integrating hardware, software, and AI, expands the Board to 11 members. Her background includes leadership roles at Google (healthcare, Google/Nest CTO, Google X co-founder), Apple, and Quanttus, as well as a distinguished academic career in AI and robotics, making her a valuable addition to ADI's mission to bridge the physical and digital worlds.

title: Greg Abel's First Significant Move Since Warren Buffett's Retirement Was Likely Just Revealed by One of Berkshire Hathaway's Largest Holdings
summary: Following Warren Buffett's retirement as CEO, Greg Abel's first significant strategic move at Berkshire Hathaway appears to be a potential major sell-off of the company's stake in Kraft Heinz, as indicated by a recent SEC filing. This move signals a departure from Buffett's reluctance to sell the position and suggests Abel might also reduce holdings in other top investments like Apple and Bank of America due to valuation concerns. Abel, a value investor, seems poised to reshape Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio through divestitures, even as the company holds significant cash reserves.
